cases.passage: The rise of e-commerce has transformed the way businesses operate globally. Companies like Amazon and Alibaba have become household names, with a massive presence in almost every country. However, the growth of e-commerce has also raised concerns about the exploitation of workers in the global supply chain. For instance, the conditions of workers in Chinese factories that produce goods for Amazon have been criticized for being inhumane. This has led to calls for greater regulation of e-commerce companies and their supply chains.

questions.q: What is the impact of e-commerce on the global supply chain?
opts: (A) It has led to better working conditions for workers
(B) It has raised concerns about the exploitation of workers
(C) It has increased the demand for skilled labor
(D) It has reduced the need for physical stores
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the growth of e-commerce has raised concerns about the exploitation of workers in the global supply chain.

questions.q: Which companies are mentioned in the passage as having a massive presence globally?
opts: (A) Amazon and Alibaba
(B) Walmart and Tesco
(C) Google and Facebook
(D) Apple and Microsoft
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that companies like Amazon and Alibaba have become household names with a massive presence in almost every country.

questions.q: What is the concern raised by the conditions of workers in Chinese factories that produce goods for Amazon?
opts: (A) They are paid well
(B) They are working in humane conditions
(C) They are being exploited
(D) They are not working at all
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the conditions of workers in Chinese factories that produce goods for Amazon have been criticized for being inhumane, leading to concerns about exploitation.

questions.q: What has been the result of the criticism of working conditions in Chinese factories that produce goods for Amazon?
opts: (A) Greater profits for Amazon
(B) Increased demand for Amazon's products
(C) Calls for greater regulation of e-commerce companies
(D) None of the above
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the criticism of working conditions has led to calls for greater regulation of e-commerce companies and their supply chains.

questions.q: What is the implication of the growth of e-commerce for businesses globally?
opts: (A) It has reduced the need for physical stores
(B) It has increased the demand for skilled labor
(C) It has transformed the way businesses operate globally
(D) It has had no impact
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the rise of e-commerce has transformed the way businesses operate globally.

cases.passage: The concept of globalisation has been debated extensively by scholars and policymakers. Some argue that it has led to greater economic growth and increased connectivity among nations. Others argue that it has resulted in the exploitation of workers and the degradation of the environment. The case of the garment industry in Bangladesh is a prime example of the negative consequences of globalisation. Workers in the industry are paid extremely low wages and work in hazardous conditions, with little protection from the law.

questions.q: What is the argument made by some scholars and policymakers about the impact of globalisation?
opts: (A) It has led to greater economic growth and increased connectivity among nations
(B) It has resulted in the exploitation of workers and the degradation of the environment
(C) It has had no impact on the economy
(D) It has led to the decline of traditional industries
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that some scholars and policymakers argue that globalisation has led to greater economic growth and increased connectivity among nations.

questions.q: What is the case of the garment industry in Bangladesh used to illustrate?
opts: (A) The positive impact of globalisation
(B) The negative consequences of globalisation
(C) The impact of trade policies on the economy
(D) The role of technology in globalisation
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the case of the garment industry in Bangladesh is a prime example of the negative consequences of globalisation.

questions.q: What are the working conditions like for workers in the garment industry in Bangladesh?
opts: (A) They are well-paid and work in safe conditions
(B) They are paid low wages and work in hazardous conditions
(C) They are not employed at all
(D) They work long hours but are paid well
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that workers in the garment industry in Bangladesh are paid extremely low wages and work in hazardous conditions.

questions.q: What is the protection afforded to workers in the garment industry in Bangladesh?
opts: (A) They have full protection from the law
(B) They have some protection from the law but it is inadequate
(C) They have no protection from the law
(D) They are not employed in the industry
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that workers in the garment industry in Bangladesh have little protection from the law.

questions.q: What is the implication of the garment industry in Bangladesh for the concept of globalisation?
opts: (A) It has led to greater economic growth and increased connectivity among nations
(B) It has resulted in the exploitation of workers and the degradation of the environment
(C) It has had no impact on the economy
(D) It has led to the decline of traditional industries
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the garment industry in Bangladesh is a prime example of the negative consequences of globalisation, including the exploitation of workers.
